MOTION / Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Motion To Withdraw As Class Representative Or, In The Alternative, To Discharge Mark Burton, Esq., As Counsel Of Record For Plaintiff [Ccp 284(2)
Off Calendar Jun-09-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Burton of Hersh & Hersh telephone number 415-441-5544 for Plaintiff.
Amanda Riddle of Corey, Luzaich, de Ghetaldi, Nastari & Riddle LLP telephone number 650-871-5666 for Plaintiff.
Plaintiff Dolores Lacanlale also present.
Caroline Carrera (Tagalog Interpreter) also present.

The Clerk administered the interpreter's oath. The matter was argued. Ms. Riddle stated that she is prepared to represent the class in this matter. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling. In re: PLAINTIFF DOLORES LACANLALE'S MOTION To Withdraw As Class Representative Or, In The Alternative, To Discharge Mark Burton, Esq., As Counsel Of Record For Plaintiff [Ccp 284(2) - Pursuant to CCP section 284(2), the court grants Dolores Lacanlale's motion to discharge Mark Burton, Esq., and Hersh & Hersh and to substitute in Amanda L. Riddle, Esq., and the law firm of Corey, Luzaich, de Ghetaldi, Nastari & Riddle LLP, as Dolores Lacanlale's counsel of record in this matter. The right to change attorneys, with or without cause, has been characterized as universal. (See Echlin v. Superior Court of San Mateo (1939) 13 Cal.2d 368.) Ms. Lacanlale's moving papers indicate that Mr. Burton is no longer representing her interests or following her wishes in this matter. (See Declaration of Amanda Riddle 1-21, Ex. D, G, H.) Permitting substitution of counsel in this matter will not prejudice absent class members because in filing any motions to dismiss the instant action, Ms. Lacanlale will be required to comply with the procedures regarding dismissal of a class action as set out in CRC 3.770 and LaSala v. American Sav. & Loan Assn. (1971) 5 Cal.3d 864. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/SB)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Feb-24-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MARK E. BURTON, JR., ESQ. of Hersh and Hersh, Tel No. 415-441-5544 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. MICHAEL A. FARBSTEIN, ESQ. of Farbstein & Blackman, Tel No. 650-554-6200 appearing for Defendant Mission Motel.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF DOLORES LACANLALE'S Motion For Protective Order is denied (without prejudice). Plaintiff cites no authority for the proposition that a court may prohibit an employer from speaking with its current employees regarding the subject matter of a pending lawsuit brought by the employee against the employer, nor is the court aware of any such authority. The relief sought by plaintiff's motion would, if granted, significantly infringe on defendant's speech rights and its rights to attempt to resolve pending litigation against it. Assuming without deciding that the requested protective order could be granted under some circumstances, plaintiff has failed to establish that, absent issuance of the requested order, plaintiff or other members of the class are at risk of imminent and serious harm to their employment or their right to pursue litigation against the defendant. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
